Q&A credit to 滑蛋豬仔飯

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

If you are getting an UK Tier 2 (General) visa and have British National (Overseas) status, you should get your UK Tier 2 visa using your BN(O) passport. This is because -

1) BN(O) passport holders with a long term UK visa do not need to register with the local police while HKSAR passport holders, as Chinese nationals, are required to register with the local police.

2) BN(O) passport holders with long term UK visas are treated as Commonwealth citizens in the UK. He/She can register to vote in all UK elections. HKSAR passport holders cannot register to vote in any UK elections. Being on the electoral roll helps you to build up your own credit history in the UK.

3) BN(O) passport holders can apply for British civil service jobs open to Commonwealth citizens once he/she has Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) while HKSAR passport holders cannot apply for British civil service jobs until he/she has become a British Citizen.

4) BN(O) passport holders can apply for registration to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA), this is a cheaper method than naturalisation. HKSAR passport holder must naturalise in order to get British Citizenship.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

A number of points for those of you who got British Citizenship via your parent and living in Hong Kong -

1) You are very likely to be a British Citizen by descent. This means that your child born outside of the UK will not automatically receive British Citizenship.

2) You can move back to the UK at any time you wanted. All you need is a valid British Citizen passport.

3) You cannot get UK welfare benefit until you lived in the UK for three months.

4) However, you can use the National Health Service (NHS) as soon as you are in the UK.

5) You will need to get a National Insurance (NI) Number. You can only apply for a NI number in the UK. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

6) You will not be entitle to UK 'home' student fees until you lived in the UK for at lease three years before the start of the course.

7) If you want to give birth in the UK in order to get British Citizenship for your child, I would suggest you to do so in Northern Ireland (Belfast for example). A child born in Northern Ireland with at lease one British Citizen parent is also entitle to Irish citizenship (as well as British Citizenship). Irish citizenship remains an EU citizenship after Brexit, which means that the child have the freedom of movement in the EU.

8) Banking - you can open UK bank accounts in Hong Kong with the HSBC or the Bank of East Asia. Please note that HSBC will charge a fee.

1) You can exchange your Hong Kong driving licence to an UK one without test in the UK as long as you pass your driving test in Hong Kong and you lived in the UK for less than 5 years.

[url]https://www.facebook.com/HongkongerinUK/posts/861886470578180:0
[/url]
2) You can renew your British passport (whether it is a British Citizen or British National (Overseas) passport) in Hong Kong online.

[url]https://www.gov.uk/overseas-passports
[/url]

replies.msg:
講居住環境 理得佢咩黨 - as people in the higher social class are more likely to vote Tory (for example people in class AB - the highest social class - 47% of them voted Tory while 37% voted Labour in 2017), the assumption is that areas elected Tory MPs are likely to be wealthier than average. You are not likely to get Tory MPs elected in places like Tower Hamlets or Liverpool - just for those who are interested, Tower Hamlets last elected a Tory MP in 1880. At that time, the vote was limited to men who own property above a certain value. While in Liverpool, all the local Labour MPs won big - out of the four MPs, three won over 80% of the total vote in 2017 while the other won 'only' 79%!

You can look around the country for more examples - in the case of Birmingham, the last Tory MP was elected by the people of Birmingham Edgbaston in 1992. In the case of Glasgow, the last Tory MP was elected by the people of Glasgow Hillhead in 1979. Both Edgbaston and Hillhead are the more affluent parts of their respected city.

replies.msg:
If you are certain that you will be applying for a Tier 5 visa by 9 July 2018, then you should apply for a CoS by post using the form below -

http://www.whs.gov.hk/res/pdf/UK_regform.pdf

You will need to send the form back to Hong Kong by post to GPO Box No 10678, Central, Hong Kong.

Since the postmark on the envelope will be class as the application date, when you send the form back to Hong Kong, tell the post office counter clerk that you would like the postage to be paid by postage stamps and ask the clerk to cancel the stamps using his counter chop.

Please note that if you are successful in getting your CoS in this round, but do not use it by 9 July 2018, you will not be allow to apply for another CoS until 9 July 2019.

Being a British Citizen is already an advantage, as your employer does not need to sponsor you for a Tier 2 (General) visa.

If you did not get your British Citizenship by being born or naturalised in the UK, you are likely to be a British Citizen by descent. A British Citizen by descent cannot automatically pass on his/her British Citizenship to his/her child born outside of the UK.

Another point - have you got a National Insurance (NI) number? If not, you should apply for one in the UK as soon as you return for settlement.

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

replies.msg:
想請教覺位如果拎t2黎英國做野 - have you got British National (Overseas) status? Remember to use your BN(O) passport to register for your British Citizenship using form B(OTA). Registration is a cheap method than naturalisation using form AN.

份糧係咪要交稅同NI - do you think you can get away from paying tax in the UK? All your tax and NI contributions are taken away from you at source before going into your bank account under the PAYE system.

已經交左NHS 好重皮 - £200 per year and you think this is a lot of money!

又驚過唔到probation - why fear if you are doing your best?

If you are thinking of trying to get a job in the UK, I would suggest you to renew your BN(O) passport. You can apply for a Tier 5 (Working Holiday) visa at any time before your 31st birthday.

HKSAR passport holders, on the other hand, need to get a Certificate of Sponsorship (CoS) from the Hong Kong Labour Department. BTW - the main round of CoS application has just finished, so you will need to wait until later this year for the second round.

The biometric residence permit is proof of the holder’s right to stay, work or study in the UK. replies.msg:
Hang Seng bank is a sister bank of the HSBC - I would ask your wife to phone the 24 hour helpline 2233 3888 for more information.

https://www.hsbc.com.hk/personal/hsbc-premier/features/premier-overseas-account.html

All I would say is that having an UK bank account opened in Hong Kong saves your wife's time and effort. You will need to wait for her proof of residence - if she was lucky enough to be allocated a NI number at her BRP, then she will not get the NI number letter that could have acted as her proof of residence in the UK.

Another thing - she can apply for a provisional driving licence as a proof of residence. If she has a valid BN(O) passport, staff at the Post Office can inspect her BN(O) passport (you can put her BN(O) passport on the form) so that her passport photo with HMPO could be use by DVLA for her provisional driving licence. On the other hand, if she only has a HKSAR passport, she will need to post her HKSAR passport to the DVLA for ID purposes.

各位大英帝國做緊嘢／將會做嘢嘅巴絲打，歡迎入嚟呢到鳩噏扮下工

Q&A credit to 滑蛋豬仔飯

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

東網透視：憑一支單簧管　香港仔入選御林軍

http://hk.on.cc/hk/bkn/cnt/news/20170812/bkn-20170812093047180-0812_00822_001.html

以香港第一人身份成爲英國御林軍樂手，David憶述，源於幾年前一次聚餐時偶遇英國陸軍音樂總監，對方提起英國御林軍樂隊招募新隊員，他持有英國國民(海外)護照(BNO)身份也可參與招聘考試，故決定一試。軍樂隊考試分兩部分，體能為先，音樂第二。對於當年33歲的他來說，體能測試是極大的挑戰，要10分半鐘內跑完2.4公里，亦要做掌上壓及仰臥起坐，首輪面試因跑步不達標而失敗。但他並沒有放棄，返港苦操3、4個月後再接受挑戰，2013年過關斬將通過所有測試，成功取錄。

身為英皇室的御林軍，薪酬福利令人關注，David則不予透露，僅指薪金水平較香港警察低一些，除普通的醫療和交通福利外，最優厚是住房福利，他與同事在倫敦市中心合住一間800至900平方呎單位，月租只需30英鎊，僅相當於300港元，相信令不少香港青年人羨慕。他現長居英國，還有一年便可入籍，目標是繼續在軍隊攀升，成爲終身職業。

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

東網透視：憑一支單簧管　香港仔入選御林軍

http://hk.on.cc/hk/bkn/cnt/news/20170812/bkn-20170812093047180-0812_00822_001.html

以香港第一人身份成爲英國御林軍樂手，David憶述，源於幾年前一次聚餐時偶遇英國陸軍音樂總監，對方提起英國御林軍樂隊招募新隊員，他持有英國國民(海外)護照(BNO)身份也可參與招聘考試，故決定一試。軍樂隊考試分兩部分，體能為先，音樂第二。對於當年33歲的他來說，體能測試是極大的挑戰，要10分半鐘內跑完2.4公里，亦要做掌上壓及仰臥起坐，首輪面試因跑步不達標而失敗。但他並沒有放棄，返港苦操3、4個月後再接受挑戰，2013年過關斬將通過所有測試，成功取錄。

身為英皇室的御林軍，薪酬福利令人關注，David則不予透露，僅指薪金水平較香港警察低一些，除普通的醫療和交通福利外，最優厚是住房福利，他與同事在倫敦市中心合住一間800至900平方呎單位，月租只需30英鎊，僅相當於300港元，相信令不少香港青年人羨慕。他現長居英國，還有一年便可入籍，目標是繼續在軍隊攀升，成爲終身職業。

replies.msg:
From my limited understanding -

Under Section 37(1)(a) of the British Nationality Act 1981, a British National (Overseas) passport holder shall have the status of a Commonwealth citizen.

https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1981/61

Under Section 4(6)(b) of the Representation of the People's Act 1983 -

http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1983/2

“qualifying Commonwealth citizen” means a Commonwealth citizen who either—

(a) ...

(b) is such a person but for the time being has (or is, by virtue of any enactment, to be treated as having) any description of such leave;

According to the Electoral Commission notes on 'Entitlement to Register' -

Meaning of ‘qualifying Commonwealth citizen’

6.15 A person who is a Commonwealth citizen is a qualifying Commonwealth citizen for registration purposes if they do not require leave to enter or remain in the UK or they do require leave to enter or remain in the UK but have been granted such leave or are treated as having been granted such leave.

6.16 Any type of leave to enter or remain is acceptable, whether indefinite, time limited or conditional.

https://www.electoralcommission.org.uk/__data/assets/pdf_file/0007/43954/Part-B-Entitlement-to-register-March-2010.pdf

Border Force Officer

Closing date: 22 Apr 2018

Location
South West, East Midlands, London, West Midlands, North East, Yorkshire and the Humber, Scotland, North West, Eastern, Wales, South East

Border Force expects the highest possible standards of professionalism and teamwork from its staff and adherence to its core values: Commitment; Discipline; Respect and Moral Courage. It requires its people to operate in sometimes difficult and pressurised environments and to make well balanced decisions. It selects through a stringent and robust recruitment process only those candidates who display these qualities.

For those who meet these demanding standards, it is a hugely rewarding and very satisfying career – and one that offers the opportunity for increasing responsibility and reward.

Border Force operates 24 hours a day, 365 days of the year. Operational staff are engaged in a variety of exciting and flexible roles – this includes checking all passengers arriving by sea or air for immigration control purposes as well as the detection and interception of prohibited goods (such as drugs, cash, tobacco, alcohol, firearms, counterfeit goods). We do this at passenger and freight controls, covering people arriving on planes, by foot, in cars and coaches and freight vehicles, as well as air freight and sea containers. Staff can also expect to be deployed on other key tasks such as Radiological Nuclear Detection roles and support strategic objectives such as counter terrorism and human trafficking, which may require working across sites rather than one location, as well as in a mobile capacity inland.

https://www.civilservicejobs.service.gov.uk/csr/index.cgi?

Saw this job in the Civil Service Job Website that some of you here may be interested -

Border Force Officer

Closing date: 22 Apr 2018

Location
South West, East Midlands, London, West Midlands, North East, Yorkshire and the Humber, Scotland, North West, Eastern, Wales, South East

Border Force expects the highest possible standards of professionalism and teamwork from its staff and adherence to its core values: Commitment; Discipline; Respect and Moral Courage. It requires its people to operate in sometimes difficult and pressurised environments and to make well balanced decisions. It selects through a stringent and robust recruitment process only those candidates who display these qualities.

For those who meet these demanding standards, it is a hugely rewarding and very satisfying career – and one that offers the opportunity for increasing responsibility and reward.

Border Force operates 24 hours a day, 365 days of the year. Operational staff are engaged in a variety of exciting and flexible roles – this includes checking all passengers arriving by sea or air for immigration control purposes as well as the detection and interception of prohibited goods (such as drugs, cash, tobacco, alcohol, firearms, counterfeit goods). We do this at passenger and freight controls, covering people arriving on planes, by foot, in cars and coaches and freight vehicles, as well as air freight and sea containers. Staff can also expect to be deployed on other key tasks such as Radiological Nuclear Detection roles and support strategic objectives such as counter terrorism and human trafficking, which may require working across sites rather than one location, as well as in a mobile capacity inland.

replies.msg:
Wish you the best of luck. As I said in my posts in the past, if you are planning to come to the UK on a Tier 2 (General) visa, you should use your BN(O) passport to get your work permit. BN(O) passport holders do have the following advantage over HKSAR passport holders in the UK -

1) BN(O) passport holders are not required to register with the local police in the UK while HKSAR passport holders, as Chinese nationals, are required to register with the local police in the UK.

2) BN(O) passport holders are class as Commonwealth citizens. This means that you are entitled to register to vote in all UK elections. By being on the electoral roll, you can build up your own credit history in the UK. HKSAR passport holders are not entitle to register to vote in any UK elections.

3) BN(O) passport holders, being Commonwealth citizens in the UK, can apply for UK civil service jobs open to Commonwealth citizens.

4) BN(O) passport holders can gain British Citizenship via registration using form B(OTA), which is a cheaper way to get British Citizenship. HKSAR passport holder must naturalised using form AN in order to get British Citizenship.
